channel.title: Blogs on Muhammad Samir Assawalhy
channel.description: Recent content in Blogs on Muhammad Samir Assawalhy

item.title: Building Arabi.js — Arabizing JavaScript
item.description:
One of my most ambitious projects was Arabi.js — a tool that transforms JavaScript syntax to support Arabic keywords. The idea was simple but powerful: what if you could write JavaScript in Arabic?

The Motivation

As someone who grew up in Egypt, I noticed that many beginner programmers struggle with the English-centric nature of programming languages. While English proficiency is important in tech, the initial barrier of learning programming concepts AND a foreign language simultaneously can be overwhelming.

item.title: My Journey in Competitive Programming
item.description:
Competitive programming has been one of the most defining experiences of my university years. It all started in my first year at Zagazig University when a senior student introduced me to the ICPC community.

The Beginning

I remember the first time I tried solving a problem on Codeforces. It was a simple “A” problem, and it took me almost an hour to solve. But the feeling of seeing “Accepted” for the first time was incredible. That moment hooked me into the world of competitive programming.
